landing.title: Fibery — Operating system for orgs run by nerds
landing.text:
Fibery — Operating system for orgs run by nerds
Solutions
Tailored for your business
Product
Turn customer insights into real products without breaking your flow.
Digital agency
Collaborate with clients, manage projects, and connect work to a built-in CRM.
Software development
Run sprints your way, manage tasks, and build a wiki that actually helps.
Startup
Invent, build, and grow — without drowning in tools you don’t need.
Looking for something different?
No-code
Design your perfect workspace from the ground up with no-code.
Add any template, any time
Sales CRM
OKR Tracking
Recruitment
View all templates →
Platform
Building blocks
Features
Explore our most powerful features that go with every plan.
Templates
Discover, install and fully customize any template in minutes.
Integrations
Get a single connected workspace where all teams are welcome.
Fibery AI
Brainstorm, automate tasks, find relevant data, and experiment with your process.

2025 in review: how we grew 85% YoY while cutting marketing spend by 85%
Operating system for orgs run by nerds
A modular work platform that replaces scattered tools and brings everything and everyone together.
Sign up
Book a Demo
Mo tools
mo problems
Notorious D.O.G.
Instead of every team and every kind of data having its own isolated tool…
Whiteboard
Pastures
Spreadsheet
Slums
Issue
Factories
Productivity
Obelisks
Document
Graveyards
…get everyone and everything together in one collaborative and interconnected workspace:
drag to explore
Startup
Enterprise
Agency
Non-profit
We're constantly surprised by the kind of orgs that run on Fibery: a self-driving boats startup, a Bible translating non-profit, a cannabis farm.
Everything is deeply
intertwingled
Ted Nelson
Fibery allows me to
break the silos between business processes
, either by replacing them with Fibery spaces or connecting them through integrations. This is an organizational quantum leap!
Mikael Klingvall
Chief Ontology Officer, Dairdux
Sure, we have templates
to get you started
Opportunity Solution Tree
JTBD
Atomic research
OKR Tracking
SPADE framework
Allocation Management
Retrospectives
Software Development
Content Marketing
Event Planning
Market research
Sales CRM
30-60-90 Onboarding
Employee Management
Hiring
Vacations
Finance
StartUp accelerator management
Project Management
Meeting Notes
Templates
But Fibery thrives when you
make it truly yours.
Deep inside, you’re
an architect, aren’t you?
Tell it how you want to run your team
— and Fibery will adapt.
Due to its modular nature it can handle whatever you throw at it.
Relational Databases
The Core
Views
The Face
Table
Board
Gallery
List
Timeline
Gantt
Calendar
Report
Map
Feed
Form
Dashboard
Hierarchy, as deep as you need
Copy-paste cells
If you excel at Excel, this will be easy
Multiple kinds of data
Swimlanes and color coding
Inline edit
Nothing to flex about
Hierarchy, as deep as you need
Inline edit
Multiple kinds of data
Actual vs. planned dates
Dependency-aware rescheduling
Collapsible hierarchy
Actual vs. planned dates
Dependency-aware rescheduling
Multiple kinds of data
Color coding
A freaking BI tool, a product inside product
Barcharts, scatterplots, tables, etc.
Based on actual or historical data
Color coding
Satellite view
Scrollable feed based on your data
Rich-text description
Required fields
Hidden pre-selected fields
Multiple Views on one screen
Docs & whiteboards
Embeds from other tools
Automations
The Machine
Formulas
The Math
Access Templates
The Keys
few building blocks to learn, infinite ways to put them together
Complex, not complicated
Features
This seems a lot
more powerful than Notion
. I'm extremely surprised this isn't extremely popular. Just wondering if I am missing something, like if the CEO tortures small kittens.
O
One dude
on Reddit
The explanation is simple: we are much better at product and engineering than at sales and marketing.
However, you are here, so it means that we are doing something right. Finally!
Oh, and also I waterboard kittens.
Michael,
Fibery
Powerful for those who can handle it,
Simple for everyone else
Teammates and volunteers don't have to know about the complexity - you can prepare familiar and simple Views for adding the data, working with that and etc.
C
Calvin G.
A kind human being
We have nuns using Fibery, for God's sake.
Architect Mode
Docs
The Swarm
Whiteboards
The Chaos
Smart Search
The Light
Mobile App
The Flash
When everything is connected,
AI finally makes sense
Since everything's in the same place and neatly organized in a relational database, AI can answer pretty challenging questions spanning multiple teams and processes:
Understanding > Productivity
Bring the data from the tools that you cherish into Fibery and connect with the rest of your workspace
Not ready to go all-in?
Integrations
Native connectors
No sweat
Integration platforms
Some sweat
Custom connectors
Real sweat
We won’t leave you alone
(unless you ask)
Polina, Chris, and Kseniya would be happy to support you in bringing any process (however weird) into Fibery.
Chat now
Schedule a call
The support team is super friendly, approachable and helpful even when it comes to assisting with complicated scenarios, solving problems, or suggestions - big ups to them!
Greg Bacchus
Chief Architect, Directed Technologies
We will annoy you
with (usually meaningful) product updates every Thursday
It's insane how fast you guys deliver & build.
Yvette Lans
CEO, Acceleright
I’m always blown away how
updates make sense
. It is well thought every time.
Comparing to my time with ClickUp this is next level s*it.
Jan Drabek
Developer, Better Marketing
In progress:
vibe-coded apps on top of Fibery, better performance on large datasets, suggestion mode in docs.
May 2026
configurable users visibility, more reliable MCP server, mixing lists in rich text.
April 2026
generate interfaces with AI, display fields from relations, developer portal.
March 2026
more capable MCP server, drag'n'drop for rich-text blocks, conditional entity views.
See changelog
The last work platform you'll switch to?
Get started
Schedule a demo
This is what I love about Fibery.
It grows with you
. As you learn new things, as you expand, as you change your workflow - Fibery is there, ready to fit your needs
Adam Fendrych
Product Manager, Outfindo
See what our customers say about Fibery — yes, even the ugly stuff

and

Support
User guide and AI support
Chat and email
support
Our team of solution architects are happy to advise you on how to map your workflows into Fibery and answer any questions you have.
Migration
assistance
We're always happy to suggest the easiest way to migrate knowledge and work from your existing tools into Fibery. For a bit of extra money, we can even take care of it for you—white gloves and all.
Chat with us
Chat with us
Chat with us
Customer success
manager
You’ll have a dedicated solution architect who understands the nuances of your workspace and provides tailored recommendations.
Billing
Option to pay by bank transfer
Available if you pay annually or the invoice amount exceeds $1000.
Multi-workspace
billing
Get a consolidated invoice and pay for each user once, regardless of how many workspaces they are in.
Custom contract
Questions & Answers
Is there a trial period?
Yes, every workspace gets a 14-day Pro plan trial. Fibery is powerful and takes time to explore. So you need time to procrastinate, receive some reminders, get into Fibery again, and, finally, "get" it. We think you can do it in 14 days. If you'd like to have a jump start, we suggest our
concierge support
.
What happens when the trial is over?
Once the trial is over, we'll ask you to make a decision: you can continue with Pro, select another paid plan, or switch to Free. No need to wait until the end of the trial period though: add billing details and select your plan at any point during the first 14 days to ensure uniterrupted experience.
Is there really no entities limit on the Free plan?
Yes, you can have any number of rows in your databases.  The performance depends on your usage patterns—for example, Tables handle large volumes of data better than Boards, and complex formulas take longer to calculate in large databases. If you have fewer than 50K entities, you'll almost certainly be fine. If you have more than 500K data points, consider aggregating them before adding to Fibery.
What about these 6 months for free for startups?
Get 6 months of Fibery for free if your company has fewer than 42 employees.
Ditch those scattered spreadsheets, and let’s grow together.
Learn more & apply
What happens if we invite/deactivate users when on a paid plan?
When you invite a new user, we charge you proportionally to the time left in the billing cycle. For example, if you pay annually and there are six months left until the next payment, the prorated amount is
$
12*(6/12)*12=
$
72.
When you deactivate a user, the unspent amount will be deducted from your next bill.
What payment methods do you accept?
We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and Apple Pay. Pro and Enterprise customers can also pay via bank transfer. Credit cards and Paypal are available via self-service at checkout. Please contact us for Apple Pay or bank transfer payments.
How is the payment being processed?
We use Chargebee at the checkout and Braintree to process your payment.
Braintree
handles billions of dollars in transactions and is trusted by companies like Uber, Dropbox, and GitHub. We do not store or handle your credit card information directly.
Do you offer refunds?
We do. If you've made a mistake while signing up for a paid subscription, accidentally added paid members to your workspace, or simply lost enthusiasm for Fibery, please reach out to us to us via chat or at
new@fibery.io
within 30 days of payment.
Can we cancel our paid plan?
Sure, feel free to cancel your subscription at any time. All the benefits of the paid

analysis.name: Fibery
analysis.tagline: Operating system for orgs run by nerds
analysis.value_proposition: A modular, relational work platform that replaces scattered tools by connecting every team, process, and data type in one interconnected no-code workspace.
analysis.ideal_customer: Tech-savvy product, software, and ops teams that want deep customization, cross-functional data linking, and a single tool instead of a stack of point solutions.

features.name: relational databases
features.description: Core data model: every entity type lives in a typed database that can be related to any other across teams and spaces.

features.name: table view
features.description: Spreadsheet-like grid with inline editing, copy-paste cells, multiple data types, and deep hierarchy.
features.evidence_quote: Copy-paste cells. If you excel at Excel, this will be easy

features.name: board view
features.description: Kanban-style board with swimlanes and color coding.

features.name: list view
features.description: Simple linear list of entities with inline editing.

features.name: timeline view
features.description: Timeline visualization with actual vs. planned dates and dependency-aware rescheduling.
features.evidence_quote: Timeline... Actual vs. planned dates. Dependency-aware rescheduling

features.name: gantt view
features.description: Gantt chart with collapsible hierarchy, actual vs. planned dates, and dependency-aware rescheduling.
features.evidence_quote: Gantt... Collapsible hierarchy. Actual vs. planned dates. Dependency-aware rescheduling

features.name: calendar view
features.description: Calendar visualization of date-based entities.

features.name: gallery view
features.description: Card/image gallery layout for entities.

features.name: map view
features.description: Geographic map view for location-based data with satellite view option.

features.name: feed view
features.description: Scrollable social-media-style feed of entities (notes, briefs, specs) with rich-text content.
features.evidence_quote: Feed. Imagine a social media feed but for your notes, briefs, or specs.

features.name: form view
features.description: Smart forms for data entry with required fields and hidden pre-selected fields.
features.evidence_quote: Forms. Smart... Required fields. Hidden pre-selected fields.

features.name: charts / bi tool
features.description: Built-in BI tool with bar charts, scatterplots, tables, color coding, and historical data queries.
features.evidence_quote: A freaking BI tool, a product inside product. Barcharts, scatterplots, tables, etc.

features.name: historical data in charts
features.description: Charts can query past-state (historical) data, not just current snapshots.

features.name: dashboards
features.description: Dashboard canvas showing multiple views and reports on a single screen.
features.evidence_quote: Dashboards... Multiple Views on one screen

features.name: whiteboards
features.description: Collaborative freeform whiteboards embedded within the workspace.

features.name: collaborative docs
features.description: Rich-text collaborative documents available on all plans.

features.name: formulas
features.description: Formula fields for computed values, available on all plans.

features.name: automations
features.description: Rule-based workflow automations across all plans; unlimited automations on Enterprise.

features.name: js automations
features.description: JavaScript-based custom automations for advanced workflow logic (Pro+).

features.name: ai assistant
features.description: AI for brainstorming, task automation, data discovery, and cross-team Q&A using the relational data model.
features.evidence_quote: Brainstorm, automate tasks, find relevant data, and experiment with your process.

features.name: fibery mcp server
features.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server enabling AI agents to connect to and query Fibery data.

features.name: smart search
features.description: Cross-workspace search spanning all connected databases and documents.

features.name: mobile app
features.description: Native mobile application for on-the-go workspace access.

features.name: browser extension
features.description: Browser extension for capturing and interacting with Fibery from the web.

features.name: template library
features.description: Pre-built workspace templates (OKRs, CRM, Sprints, Hiring, etc.) that are fully customizable post-install.
features.evidence_quote: Discover, install and fully customize any template in minutes.

features.name: native integrations
features.description: Native connectors to third-party tools (specific names not available in crawled sources).

features.name: integration platform support
features.description: Support for iPaaS platforms (e.g., Zapier, Make) to connect third-party tools.

features.name: custom api connectors
features.description: Developer-built custom connectors for bespoke integrations via API.

features.name: observers (free read-only users)
features.description: Free read-only seat type that can comment; included at no cost on all plans.
features.evidence_quote: Observers: Invite your colleagues as free, read-only users who can comment. Transparency FTW!

features.name: guests
features.description: Free external collaborator role scoped to specific entities for clients, partners, or freelancers.
features.evidence_quote: Collaborate with partners, clients, and freelancers on specific entities without having to pay

features.name: user groups
features.description: Group users for bulk permission management (Pro+).

features.name: advanced permissions
features.description: Granular role-based access control beyond basic view/edit permissions (Pro+).

features.name: sso / saml sso
features.description: SAML-based Single Sign-On for Enterprise authentication.

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: Automated user provisioning and deprovisioning via SCIM protocol (Enterprise only).

features.name: data residency
features.description: Choice of US, EU, UK, or APAC data residency region for Enterprise compliance.
features.evidence_quote: US, EU, UK, or APAC data residency

features.name: version history
features.description: Entity and doc version history: 14 days (Free), 90 days (Standard), unlimited (Pro+).
features.evidence_quote: Version history: 14 days / 90 days / Unlimited / Unlimited

features.name: entity views / layouts
features.description: Custom layouts for individual entity record pages; Pro+ adds rules-based conditional view routing.
features.evidence_quote: Entity Views: A way to customize how you see a database record when you open it.

features.name: dynamic folders
features.description: Sidebar folders that auto-organize themselves based on data dimensions like teams, objectives, or clients.
features.evidence_quote: Dynamic folders organizing the sidebar around your teams, objectives, or clients.

features.name: dependency-aware rescheduling
features.description: Automatic propagation of date changes through dependent tasks in timeline and gantt views.

features.name: cross-database relations
features.description: Native relations between databases in different Spaces for cross-team data linking without silos.
features.evidence_quote: Fibery natively supports relations between databases from different Spaces

features.name: no-code workspace builder
features.description: No-code interface to design schemas, fields, views, and relations from scratch.
features.evidence_quote: Design your perfect workspace from the ground up with no-code.

features.name: embeds from other tools
features.description: Embed content from external tools directly within Fibery docs and views.

features.name: file storage
features.description: File attachments: 1 GB on Free, unlimited on all paid plans.
features.evidence_quote: File storage: 1 GB / Unlimited / Unlimited / Unlimited

features.name: deep hierarchy
features.description: Unlimited nesting depth for parent-child entity structures across all view types.
features.evidence_quote: Hierarchy, as deep as you need

features.name: swimlanes
features.description: Horizontal swimlane grouping in board views for multi-dimensional organization.

features.name: color coding
features.description: Color coding of entities across views driven by field values.

features.name: inline editing
features.description: Direct in-place editing of entity fields within any view without opening the full record.

features.name: required fields
features.description: Mark fields as required to enforce data completeness on forms and entity records.

features.name: multiple views on one screen
features.description: Dashboard-style canvas displaying multiple view types simultaneously on a single page.

features.name: human support
features.description: Access to human support agents (Standard+; Free plan has no human support).

features.name: customer success manager
features.description: Dedicated CSM assigned to Enterprise accounts.

features.name: startup program
features.description: 6 months free on Pro plan for qualifying startups (saves up to $6,048).
features.evidence_quote: Get 6 months for free on Pro plan to invent, build, and grow your startup

features.name: non-profit / edu discount
features.description: 50% discount for non-profit, educational, and Ukrainian organizations; 100% for open-source.
features.evidence_quote: 50% discount for non-profit, educational, and Ukrainian organizations.

differentiators: Everything in one relational workspace eliminates cross-team silos — data from any team relates to any other natively
AI answers questions spanning multiple teams because all data is co-located in one relational graph
Built-in BI/chart tool with historical data queries — rare for work management tools
Observers and guests are free on all tiers, removing per-seat upsell friction for transparency
MCP server enables AI agent connectivity to live workspace data
Grew 85% YoY while cutting marketing spend by 85% (founder-stated)
Modular no-code architecture: 'few building blocks to learn, infinite ways to put them together'
Data residency choice (US, EU, UK, APAC) available at Enterprise tier
Free tier is genuinely functional, not a camouflaged trial (founder explicitly calls this out)

recent_ships.what: Changelog entry published May 28, 2026 (full content unavailable — changelog page returned 404 during crawl)
recent_ships.source: Changelog: May 28, 2026 — referenced in site nav

praised: "This seems a lot more powerful than Notion. I'm extremely surprised this isn't extremely popular." — Reddit
"Fibery allows me to break the silos between business processes... This is an organizational quantum leap!" — Mikael Klingvall, Chief Ontology Officer, Dairdux
"Fibery costs almost as much as each of the four tools that we used previously. The difference in price is obvious." — Nikita Fedoseev, enKod
"Due to its modular nature it can handle whatever you throw at it." — Calvin G.
Powerful for experts, accessible for non-technical teammates who only see simple prepared views
complaints: Steep learning curve implied: 'Powerful for those who can handle it' — adoption friction for non-power users
Discoverability/marketing weakness — founder self-acknowledged: 'we are much better at product and engineering than at sales and marketing'
2.5-hour downtime event documented in HN post — historical reliability concern
HN thread titled 'Yet Another Collaboration Tool' (705 upvotes) suggests differentiation is not immediately obvious to outsiders

free_tier.limits: 10 users + 10 guests, 10 databases, 1 GB storage, 14-day version history

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: 10 users, 10 DBs, unlimited entities, docs, formulas, automations, integrations, AI

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited DBs, AI, charts w/ historical data, whiteboards, human support, 90-day version history

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: Everything in Standard + user groups, advanced permissions, JS automations, unlimited version history

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Everything in Pro + unlimited automations/integrations, SAML SSO, SCIM, data residency, CSM

pricing.notes: All tier prices are annual billing rates (billed yearly); monthly billing available at ~20% premium. Observers and guests are free on all tiers. Enterprise requires minimum 25 paid users. Non-profit, educational, and Ukrainian orgs get 50% off; open-source projects get 100% off. Startup program: 6 months free on Pro (up to $6,048 savings). No paid add-ons per pricing page.
